SHELL        = /bin/bash
.SHELLFLAGS := -eu -o pipefail -c
PYTHON      ?= $(shell command -v python3 || command -v python)
PIP          = $(PYTHON) -m pip
NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS := pull

.PHONY: all
all:
	@cmake -S . -B build $(shell $(PYTHON) ./scripts/get_python_cmake_flags.py) && \
		cmake --build build --parallel --

.PHONY: triton
triton:
	$(PIP) uninstall -y triton
	@./scripts/install_triton_wheel.sh

.PHONY: clean
clean: # This will remove ALL build folders.
	@rm -r build*/ || true

.PHONY: linecount
linecount:
	@cloc --read-lang-def=caffe.cloc caffe2 || \
		echo "Cloc is not available on the machine. You can install cloc with " && \
		echo "    sudo apt-get install cloc"

.PHONY: ensure-branch-clean
ensure-branch-clean:
	@if [ -n "$(shell git status --porcelain)" ]; then \
		echo "Please commit or stash all changes before running this script"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi

.PHONY: setup-env
setup-env: ensure-branch-clean
	$(PYTHON) tools/nightly.py $(NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS)

.PHONY: setup-env-cuda

	$(MAKE) setup-env PYTHON="$(PYTHON)" NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS="$(NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS) --cuda"

	$(MAKE) setup-env PYTHON="$(PYTHON)" NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS="$(NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS) --rocm"

.PHONY: setup-lint

	@echo "Setting up lintrunner..."

3: import torch
4: import torch.utils._pytree as pytree
5: from torch._ops import HigherOrderOperator
6: from torch._subclasses.fake_tensor import FakeTensorMode

9: 
10: class Print(HigherOrderOperator):
11:     """
12:     print(format_str, **kwargs) -> None
13: 
14:     This Higher Order Operator (HOP) provides a functional version of print for use in PyTorch graphs.
15:     It enables format printing with named arguments, e.g., torch._higher_order_ops.print("moo {x} {y}", x=1, y=2).
16: 
17:     This HOP enables printing without causing graph break.
18:     """
19: 
20:     def __init__(self) -> None:
21:         super().__init__("print")
22: 
23:     def __call__(self, format_str: str, **kwargs: object) -> None:
24:         assert isinstance(format_str, str)
25:         return super().__call__(format_str, **kwargs)
26: 
27:     # pyrefly: ignore [bad-override]
28:     def gen_schema(self, format_str: str, **kwargs: object) -> torch.FunctionSchema:
29:         from torch._higher_order_ops.schema import HopSchemaGenerator
30: 
31:         schema_gen = HopSchemaGenerator(self)
32:         schema_gen.add_arg("format_str", format_str[0])
33: 
34:         # Add each kwarg as a keyword-only argument
35:         for key, value in kwargs.items():
36:             schema_gen.add_arg(key, value, kw_only=True)
37: 
38:         schema_gen.add_schema_tree_spec(format_str, **kwargs)
39: 
40:         return schema_gen.gen_schema()
41: 
42: 
43: print = Print()
44: 
45: 
46: @print.py_impl(ProxyTorchDispatchMode)
47: # pyre-ignore
48: def print_proxy_torch_dispatch_mode(
49:     mode: ProxyTorchDispatchMode, format_str: str, **kwargs: object
50: ) -> None:
51:     proxy_kwargs = pytree.tree_map(mode.tracer.unwrap_proxy, kwargs)  # type: ignore[union-attr]  # noqa: F841
52:     mode.tracer.create_proxy("call_function", print, (format_str,), proxy_kwargs)
53: 
54: 
55: @print.py_impl(FakeTensorMode)
56: # pyre-ignore
57: def print_fake_tensor_mode(mode, format_str: str, **kwargs: object):
58:     return None
59: 
60: 
61: @print.py_impl(torch._C.DispatchKey.CompositeExplicitAutograd)
62: # pyre-ignore
63: def print_impl(format_str: str, **kwargs: object) -> None:
64:     # Ensure all immutable_dict/list in kwargs are converted to regular dict/list
65:     map_types: dict[type, type] = {
66:         torch.fx.immutable_collections.immutable_dict: dict,
67:         torch.fx.immutable_collections.immutable_list: list,
68:     }
69:     new_kwargs = pytree.tree_map_only(
70:         tuple(map_types.keys()),
71:         lambda a: map_types[type(a)](a),
72:         kwargs,
73:         lambda a: isinstance(a, tuple(map_types.keys())),
74:     )
75:     #  Use built-in print to avoid recursion with the HOP print
76:     builtins.print(format_str.format(**new_kwargs))
77: 
78: 
79: print.fallthrough(torch._C.DispatchKey.AutogradCPU)
80: print.fallthrough(torch._C.DispatchKey.AutogradCUDA)
81: 
82: 
83: @print.py_functionalize_impl
84: def print_func(ctx, format_str: str, **kwargs: object):
85:     from torch._higher_order_ops.effects import handle_effects
86: 
87:     return handle_effects(
88:         ctx.mode._allow_token_discovery,
89:         ctx.mode._tokens,
90:         print,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
91:         (format_str,),
92:         kwargs,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
93:     )

7: from torch._higher_order_ops.print import print as hop_print
8: from torch._higher_order_ops.torchbind import call_torchbind

11: from torch._library.utils import RegistrationHandle
12: from torch._ops import HigherOrderOperator

15:     disable_proxy_modes_tracing,
16:     ProxyTorchDispatchMode,

21: _op_identifier = Union[
22:     str,
23:     "torch._ops.OpOverload",
24:     "torch._library.custom_ops.CustomOpDef",
25:     "torch._ops.HigherOrderOperator",
26: ]
27: OpType = Union["torch._ops.HigherOrderOperator", "torch._ops.OpOverload"]
28: 
29: _EffectType = EffectType
30: 
31: 
32: def _get_op_qualname(op: _op_identifier) -> str:
33:     """Convert an op identifier to a qualified string key."""
34:     if isinstance(op, torch._ops.OpOverload):
35:         return op._name
36:     elif isinstance(op, torch._ops.HigherOrderOperator):
37:         return f"{op.namespace}::{op.name()}"
38:     elif isinstance(op, CustomOpDef):
39:         return op._qualname
40:     elif isinstance(op, str):
41:         return op
42: 
43:     raise ValueError(f"Invalid operator input {op}")
44: 
45: 
46: def _register_effectful_op(
47:     op: _op_identifier, effect: Optional[EffectType]
48: ) -> RegistrationHandle:
49:     qualname = _get_op_qualname(op)
50:     entry = torch._library.simple_registry.singleton.find(qualname)
51:     handle = entry.effect.register(effect)
52:     return handle
53: 
54: 
55: def _get_effect(op: _op_identifier) -> Optional[_EffectType]:
56:     qualname = _get_op_qualname(op)
57:     entry = torch._library.simple_registry.singleton.find(qualname)
58:     return entry.effect.effect
59: 
60: 
61: _register_effectful_op("aten::_print", _EffectType.ORDERED)
62: _register_effectful_op("profiler::_record_function_exit._RecordFunction", None)
63: _register_effectful_op(call_torchbind, _EffectType.ORDERED)
64: _register_effectful_op(hop_print, _EffectType.ORDERED)
65:

67: class WithEffects(HigherOrderOperator):
68:     """
69:     with_effects(token, op, args, kwargs) -> (new_token, op_results)
70: 
71:     This HOP helps ensure ordering between side effectful ops like prints or ops
72:     using torchbind objects. This is needed to ensure a traced graph from
73:     AOTAutograd is functional so that future optimization passes do not reorder
74:     these operators. This is done through threading "effect tokens" through the
75:     graph to enforce data dependence between side effectful ops.
76: 
77:     The tokens are basically dummy values (torch.tensor([])). We create a token
78:     per "effect type", which are enumerated in the _EffectType enum.
79:     """
80: 
81:     def __init__(self) -> None:
82:         super().__init__("with_effects")
83: 
84:     def __call__(
85:         self,
86:         token,
87:         op: OpType,
88:         *args: tuple[Any, ...],
89:         **kwargs: dict[str, Any],
90:     ) -> tuple[Any, ...]:
91:         assert isinstance(op, (torch._ops.HigherOrderOperator, torch._ops.OpOverload))
92:         assert not has_aliasing(op), "Ops with aliasing is not supported"
93:         assert isinstance(kwargs, dict)
94:         return super().__call__(token, op, *args, **kwargs)
95: 
96: 
97: with_effects = WithEffects()
98: 
99: 
100: def has_aliasing(op: OpType):
101:     # NOT FOR PUBLIC USE
102:     if isinstance(op, torch._ops.HigherOrderOperator):
103:         return False
104: 
105:     for arg in op._schema.arguments:
106:         if arg.alias_info is not None:
107:             return True
108:     for arg in op._schema.returns:
109:         if arg.alias_info is not None:
110:             return True
111:     return False
112: 
113: 
114: def has_effects(op) -> bool:
115:     return (
116:         isinstance(op, (torch._ops.HigherOrderOperator, torch._ops.OpOverload))
117:         and not has_aliasing(op)
118:         and _get_effect(op) is not None
119:     )
120: 
121: 
122: def new_token_tensor() -> torch.Tensor:
123:     return torch.tensor([])
124: 
125: 
126: @with_effects.py_impl(DispatchKey.CompositeExplicitAutograd)
127: def with_effects_dense(
128:     token: torch.Tensor,
129:     op: torch._ops.OpOverload,
130:     *args: tuple[Any, ...],
131:     **kwargs: dict[str, Any],
132: ) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, ...]:
133:     out = op(*args, **kwargs)
134:     new_token = new_token_tensor()
135:     # [NOTE: with_effects return type]
136:     # Note that we should only do *out for tuple type, but not list type.
137:     # This is to match the schema of the op.
138:     # For tuple output, the length of schema output is the same as the length of out.
139:     # For list output, the length of schema output is 1 (e.g. Tensor[]) regardless of the
140:     # length of the list.
141:     if isinstance(out, tuple):

194: def _get_schema(op, args, kwargs: Optional[dict] = None) -> torch.FunctionSchema:
195:     if isinstance(op, torch._ops.OpOverload):
196:         return op._schema
197:     elif op == call_torchbind:
198:         return getattr(args[0], args[1]).schema
199:     elif op == hop_print:

201:         extra_kwargs = kwargs or {}

207: def handle_effects(
208:     allow_token_discovery: bool,
209:     tokens: dict[_EffectType, torch.Tensor],
210:     op: OpType,
211:     args: tuple[Any, ...],
212:     kwargs: dict[str, Any],
213: ) -> Any:
214:     """
215:     Args:
216:         allow_token_discovery: Whether or not we are discovering tokens. If this
217:         is true, we will create a token for every side effect type seen that
218:         does not have a token assigned yet.  If this is false, the tokens
219:         should've all been created ahead of time, so we will error if there is
220:         no token mapping to every effect type.
221: 
222:         tokens: Map of effect type to tokens. This is to chain operators of the
223:         same effects together so that they do not get reordered in later
224:         optimization passes.
225:     """
226: 
227:     # Get a token. We can't do `tokens.get(op, torch.tensor([]))` because
228:     # this will create an empty tensor during proxy mode tracing if the token
229:     # doesn't exist. But the tokens should always exist during proxy mode tracing.
230:     key = _get_effect(op)
231:     assert key is not None
232:     if key not in tokens:
233:         assert allow_token_discovery, (
234:             f"Could not find a token for effect {key} which came from the function {op}"
235:         )
236:         proxy_tensor_mode = torch._C._get_dispatch_mode(
237:             torch._C._TorchDispatchModeKey.PROXY
238:         )
239:         if proxy_tensor_mode is not None:
240:             # If we discovered a new token during tracing, we are in backward.
241:             # Then we patch the graph, adding additional tangents_token as input to the joint graph.
242:             tracer = proxy_tensor_mode.tracer
243: 
244:             from torch.fx.experimental.proxy_tensor import (
245:                 disable_proxy_modes_tracing,
246:                 track_tensor_tree,
247:             )
248: 
249:             with disable_proxy_modes_tracing():
250:                 token_tensor = new_token_tensor()
251: 
252:             token_proxy = proxy_tensor_mode.tracer.create_proxy(
253:                 "placeholder", "tangents_token", (), {}, name="tangents_token"
254:             )
255:             track_tensor_tree(token_tensor, token_proxy, constant=None, tracer=tracer)
256: 
257:             tokens[key] = token_tensor
258:         else:
259:             tokens[key] = new_token_tensor()
260: 
261:     token = tokens[key]
262: 
263:     from torch._subclasses.functional_tensor import PythonFunctionalizeAPI
264: 
265:     ctx = PythonFunctionalizeAPI()
266: 
267:     unwrapped_token = ctx.unwrap_tensors([token])[0]
268:     unwrapped_args = ctx.unwrap_tensors(args)
269:     unwrapped_kwargs = ctx.unwrap_tensors(kwargs)  # type: ignore[arg-type]
270:     with ctx.redispatch_to_next():
271:         (new_token, *unwrapped_outs) = with_effects(
272:             unwrapped_token, op, *unwrapped_args, **unwrapped_kwargs
273:         )
274: 
275:     schema = _get_schema(op, unwrapped_args, unwrapped_kwargs)
276:     if len(schema.returns) == 0:
277:         assert unwrapped_outs[0] is None
278:         unwrapped_outs = None  # type: ignore[assignment]
279:     elif len(schema.returns) == 1:
280:         assert len(unwrapped_outs) == 1
281:         unwrapped_outs = unwrapped_outs[0]
282:     else:
283:         assert len(unwrapped_outs) == len(schema.returns)
284: 
285:     # Add the newly created token into the tokens map for a following call to
286:     # use this token.
287:     wrapped_token = ctx.wrap_tensors(new_token)
288:     assert isinstance(wrapped_token, torch.Tensor)
289:     tokens[key] = wrapped_token
290: 
291:     # pyrefly: ignore [bad-argument-type]
292:     return ctx.wrap_tensors(unwrapped_outs)

318:             def record_scalar_tensor(x: torch.Tensor, prefix: str) -> None:
319:                 recorded_dict[prefix] = x.clone()

336:             class MyLinear(torch.nn.Module):
337:                 def __init__(self, in_features, out_features):
338:                     super().__init__()
339:                     self.weight = torch.nn.Parameter(
340:                         torch.randn(out_features, in_features), requires_grad=True
341:                     )
342:                     self.bias = torch.nn.Parameter(
343:                         torch.randn(out_features), requires_grad=True
344:                     )
345:

350:                 def __init__(self) -> None:
351:                     super().__init__()

360:             def forward_hook(
361:                 module: torch.nn.Module,
362:                 inputs: torch.Tensor,
363:                 output: torch.Tensor,
364:                 prefix: str,
365:                 aggregate_method: str,
366:             ) -> torch.Tensor:
367:                 if aggregate_method == "mean":
368:                     torch.ops.mylib.record_scalar_tensor(output.mean(), prefix)
369:                 elif aggregate_method == "max":
370:                     torch.ops.mylib.record_scalar_tensor(output.max(), prefix)
371:                 else:
372:                     # demo purpose, using "min"
373:                     torch.ops.mylib.record_scalar_tensor(output.sum(), prefix)

376:             def add_hooks(module, config):
377:                 handles: list[RemovableHandle] = []
378:                 q = deque([(module.__class__.__name__, module)])
379:                 while q:
380:                     name, m = q.pop()
381:                     children = [(name + "." + n, y) for (n, y) in m.named_children()]
382:                     q.extend(children)
383:                     aggregate_method = config.get(name, "mean")
384:                     prefix = name + ":" + aggregate_method
385:                     handle = m.register_forward_hook(
386:                         partial(
387:                             forward_hook,
388:                             prefix=prefix,
389:                             aggregate_method=aggregate_method,
390:                         )
391:                     )
392:                     if handle:
393:                         handles.append(handle)
394:                 return handles

    67→class WithEffects(HigherOrderOperator):
    68→    """
    69→    with_effects(token, op, args, kwargs) -> (new_token, op_results)
    70→
    71→    This HOP helps ensure ordering between side effectful ops like prints or ops
    72→    using torchbind objects. This is needed to ensure a traced graph from
    73→    AOTAutograd is functional so that future optimization passes do not reorder
    74→    these operators. This is done through threading "effect tokens" through the
    75→    graph to enforce data dependence between side effectful ops.
    76→
    77→    The tokens are basically dummy values (torch.tensor([])). We create a token
    78→    per "effect type", which are enumerated in the _EffectType enum.
    79→    """
    80→
    81→    def __init__(self) -> None:
    82→        super().__init__("with_effects")
    83→
    84→    def __call__(
    85→        self,
    86→        token,
    87→        op: OpType,
    88→        *args: tuple[Any, ...],
    89→        **kwargs: dict[str, Any],
    90→    ) -> tuple[Any, ...]:
    91→        assert isinstance(op, (torch._ops.HigherOrderOperator, torch._ops.OpOverload))
    92→        assert not has_aliasing(op), "Ops with aliasing is not supported"
    93→        assert isinstance(kwargs, dict)
    94→        return super().__call__(token, op, *args, **kwargs)
    95→
    96→
    97→with_effects = WithEffects()
    98→

   100→def has_aliasing(op: OpType):
   101→    # NOT FOR PUBLIC USE
   102→    if isinstance(op, torch._ops.HigherOrderOperator):
   103→        return False
   104→
   105→    for arg in op._schema.arguments:
   106→        if arg.alias_info is not None:
   107→            return True
   108→    for arg in op._schema.returns:
   109→        if arg.alias_info is not None:
   110→            return True
   111→    return False
   112→
   113→
   114→def has_effects(op) -> bool:
   115→    return (
   116→        isinstance(op, (torch._ops.HigherOrderOperator, torch._ops.OpOverload))
   117→        and not has_aliasing(op)
   118→        and _get_effect(op) is not None
   119→    )

   122→def new_token_tensor() -> torch.Tensor:
   123→    return torch.tensor([])
   124→
   125→
   126→@with_effects.py_impl(DispatchKey.CompositeExplicitAutograd)
   127→def with_effects_dense(
   128→    token: torch.Tensor,
   129→    op: torch._ops.OpOverload,
   130→    *args: tuple[Any, ...],
   131→    **kwargs: dict[str, Any],
   132→) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, ...]:
   133→    out = op(*args, **kwargs)
   134→    new_token = new_token_tensor()
   135→    # [NOTE: with_effects return type]
   136→    # Note that we should only do *out for tuple type, but not list type.
   137→    # This is to match the schema of the op.
   138→    # For tuple output, the length of schema output is the same as the length of out.
   139→    # For list output, the length of schema output is 1 (e.g. Tensor[]) regardless of the
   140→    # length of the list.
   141→    if isinstance(out, tuple):
   142→        return (new_token, *out)
   143→    return (new_token, out)
   144→
   145→
   146→@with_effects.py_impl(FakeTensorMode)
   147→def with_effects_fake(
   148→    mode,
   149→    token: torch.Tensor,
   150→    op: torch._ops.OpOverload,
   151→    *args: tuple[Any, ...],
   152→    **kwargs: dict[str, Any],
   153→) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, ...]:
   154→    with mode:
   155→        result = with_effects_dense(token, op, *args, **kwargs)

   159→@with_effects.py_impl(ProxyTorchDispatchMode)

   161→    mode,
   162→    token: torch.Tensor,
   163→    op: torch._ops.OpOverload,

   172→    proxy_kwargs = pytree.tree_map(mode.tracer.unwrap_proxy, kwargs)

   177→    # don't have output or their outputs are not used.
   178→    has_side_effect(op)

   180→    out_proxy = mode.tracer.create_proxy(
   181→        "call_function",
   182→        with_effects,
   183→        (proxy_token, op, *proxy_args),
   184→        proxy_kwargs,
   185→    )

   194→def _get_schema(op, args, kwargs: Optional[dict] = None) -> torch.FunctionSchema:
   195→    if isinstance(op, torch._ops.OpOverload):
   196→        return op._schema
   197→    elif op == call_torchbind:
   198→        return getattr(args[0], args[1]).schema
   199→    elif op == hop_print:
   200→        # hop_print currently expects (format_str, *kwargs) as its arguments
   201→        extra_kwargs = kwargs or {}
   202→        return op.gen_schema(*args, **extra_kwargs)
   203→    else:
   204→        raise RuntimeError(f"Unable to get schema for op {op}")

   207→def handle_effects(
   208→    allow_token_discovery: bool,
   209→    tokens: dict[_EffectType, torch.Tensor],
   210→    op: OpType,
   211→    args: tuple[Any, ...],
   212→    kwargs: dict[str, Any],
   213→) -> Any:
   214→    """
   215→    Args:
   216→        allow_token_discovery: Whether or not we are discovering tokens. If this
   217→        is true, we will create a token for every side effect type seen that
   218→        does not have a token assigned yet.  If this is false, the tokens
   219→        should've all been created ahead of time, so we will error if there is
   220→        no token mapping to every effect type.
   221→
   222→        tokens: Map of effect type to tokens. This is to chain operators of the
   223→        same effects together so that they do not get reordered in later
   224→        optimization passes.
   225→    """
   226→
   227→    # Get a token. We can't do `tokens.get(op, torch.tensor([]))` because
   228→    # this will create an empty tensor during proxy mode tracing if the token
   229→    # doesn't exist. But the tokens should always exist during proxy mode tracing.
   230→    key = _get_effect(op)
   231→    assert key is not None
   232→    if key not in tokens:
   233→        assert allow_token_discovery, (
   234→            f"Could not find a token for effect {key} which came from the function {op}"
   235→        )
   236→        proxy_tensor_mode = torch._C._get_dispatch_mode(
   237→            torch._C._TorchDispatchModeKey.PROXY
   238→        )
   239→        if proxy_tensor_mode is not None:
   240→            # If we discovered a new token during tracing, we are in backward.
   241→            # Then we patch the graph, adding additional tangents_token as input to the joint graph.
   242→            tracer = proxy_tensor_mode.tracer
   243→
   244→            from torch.fx.experimental.proxy_tensor import (
   245→                disable_proxy_modes_tracing,
   246→                track_tensor_tree,
   247→            )
   248→
   249→            with disable_proxy_modes_tracing():
   250→                token_tensor = new_token_tensor()
   251→
   252→            token_proxy = proxy_tensor_mode.tracer.create_proxy(
   253→                "placeholder", "tangents_token", (), {}, name="tangents_token"
   254→            )
   255→            track_tensor_tree(token_tensor, token_proxy, constant=None, tracer=tracer)
   256→
   257→            tokens[key] = token_tensor
   258→        else:
   259→            tokens[key] = new_token_tensor()
   260→
   261→    token = tokens[key]
   262→
   263→    from torch._subclasses.functional_tensor import PythonFunctionalizeAPI
   264→
   265→    ctx = PythonFunctionalizeAPI()
   266→
   267→    unwrapped_token = ctx.unwrap_tensors([token])[0]
   268→    unwrapped_args = ctx.unwrap_tensors(args)
   269→    unwrapped_kwargs = ctx.unwrap_tensors(kwargs)  # type: ignore[arg-type]
   270→    with ctx.redispatch_to_next():
   271→        (new_token, *unwrapped_outs) = with_effects(
   272→            unwrapped_token, op, *unwrapped_args, **unwrapped_kwargs
   273→        )
   274→
   275→    schema = _get_schema(op, unwrapped_args, unwrapped_kwargs)
   276→    if len(schema.returns) == 0:
   277→        assert unwrapped_outs[0] is None
   278→        unwrapped_outs = None  # type: ignore[assignment]
   279→    elif len(schema.returns) == 1:
   280→        assert len(unwrapped_outs) == 1
   281→        unwrapped_outs = unwrapped_outs[0]
   282→    else:
   283→        assert len(unwrapped_outs) == len(schema.returns)
   284→
   285→    # Add the newly created token into the tokens map for a following call to
   286→    # use this token.
   287→    wrapped_token = ctx.wrap_tensors(new_token)
   288→    assert isinstance(wrapped_token, torch.Tensor)
   289→    tokens[key] = wrapped_token
   290→
   291→    # pyrefly: ignore [bad-argument-type]
   292→    return ctx.wrap_tensors(unwrapped_outs)

If this is false, the tokens\n        should've all been created ahead of time, so we will error if there is\n        no token mapping to every effect type.\n\n        tokens: Map of effect type to tokens. .PHONY: all
all:
	@cmake -S . -B build $(shell $(PYTHON) ./scripts/get_python_cmake_flags.py) && \
		cmake --build build --parallel --

.PHONY: triton
triton:
	$(PIP) uninstall -y triton
	@./scripts/install_triton_wheel.sh

.PHONY: clean
clean: # This will remove ALL build folders.
	@rm -r build*/ || true

.PHONY: linecount
linecount:
	@cloc --read-lang-def=caffe.cloc caffe2 || \
		echo "Cloc is not available on the machine. You can install cloc with " && \
		echo "    sudo apt-get install cloc"

.PHONY: ensure-branch-clean
ensure-branch-clean:
	@if [ -n "$(shell git status --porcelain)" ]; then \
		echo "Please commit or stash all changes before running this script"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi

.PHONY: setup-env
setup-env: ensure-branch-clean
	$(PYTHON) tools/nightly.py $(NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS)

.PHONY: setup-env-cuda
setup-env-cuda:
	$(MAKE) setup-env PYTHON="$(PYTHON)" NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS="$(NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS) --cuda"

.PHONY: setup-env-rocm
setup-env-rocm:
	$(MAKE) setup-env PYTHON="$(PYTHON)" NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS="$(NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS) --rocm"

.PHONY: setup-lint
setup-lint .lintbin/.lintrunner.sha256: requirements.txt pyproject.toml .lintrunner.toml
	@echo "Setting up lintrunner..."
	$(PIP) install lintrunner
	lintrunner init
	@echo "Generating .lintrunner.sha256..."
--
.PHONY: lazy-setup-lint
lazy-setup-lint: .lintbin/.lintrunner.sha256
	@if [ ! -x "$(shell command -v lintrunner)" ]; then \
		$(MAKE) setup-lint; \
	fi

.PHONY: lint
lint: lazy-setup-lint
	lintrunner --all-files

	lintrunner

	lintrunner --apply-patches

PIP          = $(PYTHON) -m pip
NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS := pull

.PHONY: all
all:
	@cmake -S . -B build $(shell $(PYTHON) ./scripts/get_python_cmake_flags.py) && \
		cmake --build build --parallel --

.PHONY: triton
triton:
	$(PIP) uninstall -y triton
	@./scripts/install_triton_wheel.sh

.PHONY: clean
clean: # This will remove ALL build folders.
	@rm -r build*/ || true

.PHONY: linecount
linecount:
	@cloc --read-lang-def=caffe.cloc caffe2 || \
		echo "Cloc is not available on the machine. You can install cloc with " && \
		echo "    sudo apt-get install cloc"

.PHONY: ensure-branch-clean
ensure-branch-clean:
	@if [ -n "$(shell git status --porcelain)" ]; then \
		echo "Please commit or stash all changes before running this script"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi

.PHONY: setup-env
setup-env: ensure-branch-clean
	$(PYTHON) tools/nightly.py $(NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS)

.PHONY: setup-env-cuda
setup-env-cuda:
	$(MAKE) setup-env PYTHON="$(PYTHON)" NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS="$(NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS) --cuda"

.PHONY: setup-env-rocm
setup-env-rocm:
	$(MAKE) setup-env PYTHON="$(PYTHON)" NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS="$(NIGHTLY_TOOL_OPTS) --rocm"

.PHONY: setup-lint
setup-lint .lintbin/.lintrunner.sha256: requirements.txt pyproject.toml .lintrunner.toml
	@echo "Setting up lintrunner..."
	$(PIP) install lintrunner
	lintrunner init
	@echo "Generating .lintrunner.sha256..."
	@mkdir -p .lintbin
	@sha256sum requirements.txt pyproject.toml .lintrunner.toml > .lintbin/.lintrunner.sha256

.PHONY: lazy-setup-lint
lazy-setup-lint: .lintbin/.lintrunner.sha256
	@if [ ! -x "$(shell command -v lintrunner)" ]; then \
		$(MAKE) setup-lint; \
	fi

.PHONY: lint
lint: lazy-setup-lint
	lintrunner --all-files

.PHONY: quicklint
quicklint: lazy-setup-lint
	lintrunner

.PHONY: quickfix
quickfix: lazy-setup-lint
	lintrunner --apply-patches

.PHONY: test
test:
	$(PYTHON) test/higher_order_ops/test_print.py

# Deprecated target aliases
.PHONY: setup_env setup_env_cuda setup_env_rocm setup_lint
setup_env: setup-env
setup_env_cuda: setup-env-cuda
setup_env_rocm: setup-env-rocm
setup_lint: setup-lint
